title: NBA Daily Rundown: March 12, 2026
subtitle: Adebayo's Historic Night, Leonard's Dominance & Key Injury Updates

items.headline: Bam Adebayo Explodes for Historic 83 Points!
items.summary: Miami Heat center Bam Adebayo delivered a performance for the ages Tuesday night, dropping 83 points against the Washington Wizards in a 150-129 victory. His incredible outburst marks the second-highest single-game scoring total in NBA history, surpassed only by Wilt Chamberlain's 100-point game. Adebayo also set new Heat records for highest-scoring game and highest-scoring quarter.

items.headline: Star Injury Woes: Curry Out 10+ Days, Maxey Sidelined 3 Weeks
items.summary: The Golden State Warriors received a tough blow with Stephen Curry slated to miss at least another 10 days due to a persistent right knee injury. Meanwhile, Philadelphia 76ers All-Star guard Tyrese Maxey will be sidelined for at least three weeks with a finger tendon injury on his right hand. Utah Jazz guard Keyonte George also exited last night's game with a hamstring injury.

items.headline: Hornets Sting Kings in Sacramento
items.summary: LaMelo Ball dropped 30 points, Miles Bridges added 26, and rookie Kon Knueppel impressed with 24 as the Hornets defeated the Kings 117-109. DeMar DeRozan led Sacramento with 39 points, passing Tim Duncan on the NBA's all-time scoring list.

items.headline: Kawhi's 45 Powers Clippers Past Wolves
items.summary: Kawhi Leonard exploded for 45 points on 15-of-20 shooting, including six 3-pointers, leading the Clippers to a dominant 153-128 victory over the Timberwolves. Anthony Edwards paced Minnesota with 36 points.

items.headline: Knicks Rally to Defeat Jazz
items.summary: Jalen Brunson's 28 points and Jordan Clarkson's 27 points fueled the Knicks' comeback win against the Jazz, 134-117. Brice Sensabaugh led Utah with 29 points.

items.headline: Magic Extend Win Streak Over Cavs
items.summary: Desmond Bane's 35 points, including crucial late-game shots, guided the Magic to their fifth straight victory, a 128-122 win over the Cavaliers. Paolo Banchero contributed 25 points, 8 rebounds, and 7 assists. James Harden led Cleveland with 30 points.

items.headline: Pelicans Down Raptors
items.summary: Trey Murphy III scored a game-high 28 points, and Dejounte Murray added 27 as the Pelicans secured a 109-93 victory over the Raptors. Immanuel Quickley led Toronto with 25 points.

items.headline: Jokic Records 25th Triple-Double in Nuggets Win
items.summary: Nikola Jokic notched his 25th triple-double of the season as the Denver Nuggets routed the Houston Rockets.

items.headline: Pistons Snap Skid Against Nets (March 10)
items.summary: Jalen Duren scored 26 points and Cade Cunningham tallied 21 points and 15 assists as the Pistons snapped their four-game skid, dominating the Nets 138-100 on Tuesday night.

items.headline: Avdija's Strong Showing in Blazers Loss (March 10)
items.summary: Deni Avdija posted 22 points, 4 rebounds, and 7 assists for the Trail Blazers in a close 103-101 loss to the Hornets on Tuesday.

items.headline: Bam Adebayo's Monumental Effort
items.summary: Bam Adebayo's 83-point explosion is the second-highest scoring game in NBA history.

items.headline: Kawhi Leonard Dominates
items.summary: Kawhi Leonard was unstoppable, scoring 45 points on incredibly efficient shooting (75% from the field).

items.headline: DeMar DeRozan Climbs Scoring List
items.summary: DeMar DeRozan’s 39 points moved him past Tim Duncan into 18th place on the NBA’s career scoring list.

items.headline: Jokic's 25th Triple-Double
items.summary: Nikola Jokic recorded his 25th triple-double of the season in the Nuggets' rout of the Rockets.

items.headline: Cade Cunningham's Double-Double
items.summary: Cade Cunningham orchestrated the Pistons' offense with a strong scoring and assist performance.

items.headline: Deni Avdija's All-Around Game
items.summary: Israeli forward Deni Avdija showcased his versatility with 22 points and 7 assists for Portland.

items.headline: Rookie Kon Knueppel Shines
items.summary: Hornets rookie Kon Knueppel had a standout performance, contributing 24 points in the win over the Kings.

items.headline: Western Conference Playoff Race Tightens
items.summary: The Minnesota Timberwolves have slipped to sixth in the Western Conference. Meanwhile, the Los Angeles Clippers are heating up, moving above .500 with a 33-32 record after their recent win.

items.headline: Eastern Conference Shifts
items.summary: The Orlando Magic's five-game winning streak has propelled them into a tie for fifth place in the Eastern Conference alongside the Toronto Raptors. The Cleveland Cavaliers hold steady in fourth, three games ahead.

items.headline: NBA Schedule: Games for March 12, 2026
items.summary: Tonight's action includes the Atlanta Hawks vs. Brooklyn Nets, Los Angeles Lakers vs. Chicago Bulls, Miami Heat vs. Milwaukee Bucks, Orlando Magic vs. Washington Wizards, San Antonio Spurs vs. Denver Nuggets, Memphis Grizzlies vs. Philadelphia 76ers, Toronto Raptors vs. Houston Rockets, Charlotte Hornets vs. Portland Trail Blazers, and Indiana Pacers vs. Sacramento Kings.
